package com.example.config.service;

import java.beans.XMLDecoder;
import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream;
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
import java.util.Arrays;
import java.util.HashSet;
import java.util.Set;

import com.example.config.bean.UserPreferences;

/**
 * Service for loading application configurations.
 * This service provides methods to interpret and load configuration data.
 */
public class ConfigurationLoaderService {

    /**
     * Whitelist of classes allowed for deserialization.
     * Only these specific classes can be instantiated during XML decoding
     * to prevent deserialization of arbitrary malicious objects.
     */
    private static final Set<String> ALLOWED_CLASSES = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(
        "com.example.config.bean.UserPreferences",
        "java.lang.String",
        "java.lang.Integer",
        "java.lang.Boolean",
        "java.lang.Double",
        "java.lang.Float",
        "java.lang.Long",
        "java.lang.Short",
        "java.lang.Byte",
        "java.lang.Character",
        "java.lang.Object",
        "java.util.ArrayList",
        "java.util.LinkedList",
        "java.util.HashMap",
        "java.util.LinkedHashMap",
        "java.util.TreeMap",
        "java.util.HashSet",
        "java.util.TreeSet",
        "java.util.LinkedHashSet",
        "java.util.Date",
        "java.sql.Date",
        "java.sql.Time",
        "java.sql.Timestamp",
        "java.math.BigDecimal",
        "java.math.BigInteger",
        "java.util.UUID",
        "java.net.URI",
        "java.util.Locale",
        "java.util.Currency",
        "java.util.TimeZone",
        "int",
        "boolean",
        "double",
        "float",
        "long",
        "short",
        "byte",
        "char"
    ));

    /**
     * Loads an object configuration from an XML string.
     * The method deserializes the XML content into a Java object using
     * a restricted ClassLoader to prevent deserialization vulnerabilities.
     *
     * @param xmlData A string containing the XML representation of the configuration object.
     * @return The deserialized configuration object.
     * @throws SecurityException if the XML contains unauthorized class references.
     * @throws ClassNotFoundException if a class cannot be found or is not allowed.
     */
    public Object loadConfiguration(String xmlData) throws ClassNotFoundException {
        // Create a custom ClassLoader that restricts which classes can be loaded
        // This prevents instantiation of dangerous classes like ProcessBuilder, Runtime, etc.
        ClassLoader safeClassLoader = new SafeClassLoader();
        
        // Create a decoder with the safe ClassLoader to restrict deserialization
        XMLDecoder decoder = new XMLDecoder(
            new ByteArrayInputStream(xmlData.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8)),
            null,  // owner
            null,  // exception listener
            safeClassLoader
        );
        
        try {
            // Read the deserialized object
            Object result = decoder.readObject();
            
            // Defense-in-depth: verify the object is of the expected type
            if (!(result instanceof UserPreferences)) {
                throw new SecurityException("Deserialized object is not of allowed type: " + 
                    (result != null ? result.getClass().getName() : "null"));
            }
            
            return result;
        } finally {
            // Ensure the decoder is closed to free resources
            decoder.close();
        }
    }
    
    /**
     * Custom ClassLoader that implements a whitelist approach to prevent
     * loading of arbitrary classes during XML deserialization.
     */
    private static class SafeClassLoader extends ClassLoader {
        
        public SafeClassLoader() {
            super(Thread.currentThread().getContextClassLoader());
        }
        
        @Override
        public Class<?> loadClass(String name) throws ClassNotFoundException {
            // Handle primitive types - they cannot be loaded by a ClassLoader
            // but are valid class names that may appear in XML deserialization
            if ("int".equals(name)) return int.class;
            if ("boolean".equals(name)) return boolean.class;
            if ("double".equals(name)) return double.class;
            if ("float".equals(name)) return float.class;
            if ("long".equals(name)) return long.class;
            if ("short".equals(name)) return short.class;
            if ("byte".equals(name)) return byte.class;
            if ("char".equals(name)) return char.class;
            if ("void".equals(name)) return void.class;
            
            // Handle array type descriptors (e.g., [Ljava.lang.String;, [I, etc.)
            String classNameToCheck = name;
            
            if (name.startsWith("[")) {
                // Find the component type of the array
                int startIndex = name.lastIndexOf('[') + 1;
                if (startIndex < name.length()) {
                    char type = name.charAt(startIndex);
                    if (type == 'L') {
                        // Object array: [L<classname>;
                        classNameToCheck = name.substring(startIndex + 1, name.length() - 1);
                    } else {
                        // Primitive array type code (I, Z, B, etc.) - allowed
                        return super.loadClass(name);
                    }
                }
            }
            
            // Check if the class is in the whitelist
            if (!ALLOWED_CLASSES.contains(classNameToCheck)) {
                throw new ClassNotFoundException("Deserialization of class '" + name + 
                    "' is not allowed for security reasons");
            }
            
            // Delegate to parent class loader for allowed classes
            return super.loadClass(name);
        }
    }
}
